name: research
description: v2.0.3 - Clarifies methods reproducibility vs provenance/evidence integrity and fixes multiple-testing correction behavior. Breaking change from v1.x.

interface.shortDescription: Agent-driven R&D protocol for basic/applied/development research with claim and analysis discipline
interface.longDescription: v2.0.3 - A protocol skill for agent-driven R&D across the three Frascati categories: basic research (understanding phenomena, building baselines), applied research (achieving measurable objectives), and experimental development (building working systems). Clarifies that categories are chosen by plan purpose, intended use, output, and uncertainty type, not by origin or later innovation claims. Enforces shared vocabulary, Divergence checkpoint before execution, PASS/REWORK/INVALID research-review gate before claims/decisions/reports, Toulmin-derived claim structure with explicit alternatives and conditions, analysis discipline (EDA + result analysis + depth stop conditions), and human-readable reports with required figures. Includes a quant-research extension for time-series and statistically rigorous quantitative R&D (purged CV, multiple testing, leakage detection). Methods reproducibility is enforced; experiment-level replicability (env locks, commit pins) is the agent's discretion, while provenance and claim-to-artifact checks are treated as audit/evidence-integrity controls. Multiple-testing helpers use inclusive rejection boundaries and corrected Holm monotonic adjusted p-values. Breaking change from v1.x.
defaultPrompt: Design an R&D plan for this investigation
Help me decide what to do after this experimental result
Write a human-readable report from this plan and runs
